{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "IT Network Engineer",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: IT Network Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Department: Information Technology / IT Infrastructure
Reports To: IT Manager / Head of Infrastructure

About the Role


	We are seeking a skilled and proactive IT Engineer with 3&ndash;5 years of hands-on experience in network administration and systems management.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for designing, implementing, securing, and maintaining the organization&rsquo;s IT infrastructure to ensure high availability, optimal performance, and business continuity across all systems and networks.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong troubleshooting abilities, enterprise infrastructure experience, and a solid understanding of networking, virtualization, security, and cloud technologies.
	You will collaborate with internal teams and external vendors to support day-to-day IT operations while driving infrastructure improvements aligned with organizational objectives.


Key Responsibilities
Network Administration:


	Design, configure, and maintain LAN/WAN infrastructure, VPNs, firewalls, switches, and routing environments.
	Monitor network performance and troubleshoot connectivity or latency issues to minimize downtime.
	Manage enterprise networking devices including routers, switches, wireless controllers, and load balancers (Cisco, Juniper, Fortinet, or equivalent).
	Implement and enforce network security policies, ACLs, VLAN segmentation, and access controls.
	Conduct routine network audits, firmware upgrades, capacity planning, and infrastructure documentation updates.
	Ensure secure and reliable remote connectivity for hybrid or distributed work environments.


Systems Administration:


	Install, configure, and maintain Windows Server and Linux-based operating systems.
	Administer Active Directory, Group Policy, DNS, DHCP, file/print services, and user access management.
	Manage virtualization platforms such as VMware vSphere, Hyper-V, or Proxmox.
	Oversee backup, recovery, and disaster recovery processes to ensure data integrity and business continuity.
	Perform system patching, monitoring, optimization, and performance tuning.
	Support server migrations, upgrades, and infrastructure expansion projects.


IT Operations &amp; Technical Support:


	Provide Tier 2/3 support for escalated infrastructure incidents and technical service requests.
	Manage IT assets, hardware inventory, software licensing, and lifecycle tracking.
	Develop and maintain technical documentation, network diagrams, SOPs, and operational runbooks.
	Coordinate with vendors and service providers regarding procurement, maintenance, SLAs, and renewals.
	Participate in IT project implementation, change management activities, and periodic on-call support rotations.
	Support end-user productivity and ensure timely resolution of infrastructure-related issues.


Security &amp; Compliance:


	Implement and manage endpoint protection, SIEM platforms, IDS/IPS solutions, and security monitoring tools.
	Conduct vulnerability assessments and remediate identified infrastructure risks.
	Ensure compliance with internal security standards and relevant frameworks such as ISO 27001, NDPR, or GDPR.
	Support incident response activities including root cause analysis and post-incident reporting.
	Maintain secure access controls, authentication policies, and data protection measures.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electrical Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Relevant professional certifications combined with strong practical experience may be considered in place of formal education.


Experience:


	3&ndash;5 years&rsquo; experience in IT infrastructure, systems administration, or network engineering roles.
	Proven experience managing enterprise-level servers, networks, and virtualization environments.
	Experience supporting multi-site or 100+ user environments is an added advantage.


Technical Skills:
Networking:


	
	Strong understanding of TCP/IP, VLANs, BGP, OSPF, MPLS, SD-WAN, QoS, and VPN technologies (IPSec/SSL).
	


Systems Administration:


	Windows Server 2016/2019/2022
	Active Directory &amp; Group Policy
	Linux administration (Ubuntu/RHEL)
	PowerShell and Bash scripting


Virtualization:


	VMware ESXi/vCenter
	Microsoft Hyper-V
	Other enterprise hypervisor platforms


Cloud Technologies (Desirable):


	AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud Platform
	Hybrid cloud networking and infrastructure integration


Monitoring &amp; Security Tools:


	PRTG, SolarWinds, Nagios, Zabbix, or equivalent monitoring solutions
	SIEM and endpoint security platforms


Storage &amp; Backup:


	SAN/NAS administration
	RAID configuration
	Backup solutions such as Veeam or Backup Exec


Preferred Certifications:


	CompTIA Network+ or Security+
	Cisco CCNA or CCNP
	Microsoft Azure Administrator Associate, MCSA, or MCSE
	VMware VCP-DCV
	ITIL Foundation Certification
	Certifications in progress will also be considered.


Core Competencies:


	Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
	High attention to detail and documentation standards
	Ability to manage multiple priorities in fast-paced environments
	Strong teamwork and collaboration skills
	Proactive mindset with commitment to continuous learning and improvement.


What We Offer


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives
	Structured career development and growth opportunities
	Professional training and certification support
	Health insurance and pension benefits
	Paid leave and employee wellness support
	Exposure to enterprise-grade technologies and infrastructure projects
	Collaborative and innovation-driven work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send in their CVs to: cv@ascentech.com.ng using &quot;IT NETWORK ENGINEER&quot; using the Job Position as the suubject of the mail.",

  "description": "Oilserv Limited is a leading provider of integrated engineering, procurement, construction, installation &amp; commissioning (EPCIC) services and complementary solutions across the asset and program life cycle within the onshore, offshore and subsea market segments of the oil and gas sector. Oilserv Limited was incorporated in 1992 and commenced operations in 1995.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head, Contract Management

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Key Deliverables


	The Head, Contract Management is responsible for the strategic management of all client contracts, consortium agreements, subcontract agreements, and commercial obligations across Oilserv&rsquo;s EPCIC portfolio.
	The role safeguards the company&rsquo;s commercial position by ensuring contractual compliance, proactive claims management, disciplined change control, and effective dispute mitigation throughout the project lifecycle.
	The incumbent will establish enterprise-wide contract governance frameworks aligned with international EPC contracting standards, Nigerian regulatory requirements, and Oilserv&rsquo;s operational risk management objectives.


Key Roles &amp; Responsibilities
Contract Strategy &amp; Commercial Governance:


	Lead the development, review, negotiation, and administration of complex EPCIC contracts, framework agreements, service contracts, and subcontract packages.
	Establish contract management procedures, templates, delegation matrices, and governance protocols across all projects.
	Ensure contractual terms appropriately allocate risk, protect margins, and align with approved commercial strategies.
	Advise executive management on contractual exposure, liabilities, and commercial risk implications.


Claims, Variations &amp; Dispute Management:


	Drive proactive management of variations, extension of time (EOT) claims, compensation events, and contractual notices.
	Coordinate preparation and defence of claims relating to delays, disruptions, scope growth, productivity losses, and liquidated damages.
	Support dispute avoidance through structured negotiation and commercial resolution mechanisms.
	Interface with Legal, Project Controls, Planning, and Finance teams in dispute adjudication, arbitration, or litigation support.


Project Commercial Management:


	Ensure strict implementation of contractual obligations, deliverables, milestones, and commercial reporting requirements.
	Monitor contract performance indicators including revenue leakage, claims exposure, retention status, guarantees, bonds, and insurance compliance.
	Review project correspondence to ensure preservation of contractual rights and entitlements.
	Support bid clarification, tender review, and commercial evaluation processes during project acquisition stages.


Subcontract &amp; Supply Chain Oversight:


	Ensure subcontract agreements to maintain appropriate back-to-back obligations with prime contracts.
	Overseesubcontractors commercial performance, payment certifications, claims administration, and compliance obligations.
	Collaborate with Procurement and SCM teams to strengthen commercial controls across the supply chain.


Compliance, Risk &amp; Regulatory Assurance:


	Ensure compliance with the Nigerian Oil &amp; Gas Industry Content Development (NOGICD) Act, DPR / NUPRC requirements, fiscal regulations, and client contractual standards.
	Maintain alignment with international contracting frameworks including FIDIC, LOGIC, NEC, and bespoke oil &amp; gas contract structures.
	Support internal and external audits relating to contract administration and commercial governance.
	Contribute to enterprise risk management processes relating to contractual and commercial exposure.


Leadership &amp; Capability Development:


	Build and mentor a high-performing contract management team across corporate and project environments.
	Develop commercial awareness and contract administration capability across project delivery teams.
	Drive digitalisation of contract administration processes through integration with ERP and project controls systems.


Job Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Law, Quantity Surveying, Engineering, Commercial Management, or related discipline.
	MBA, master&rsquo;s degree, or professional commercial qualification is an advantage.
	Preferred:
	
		IACCM / WorldCC Certification
		RICS
		CIArb
		PMP or equivalent commercial/project certification.
	
	
	12 &ndash; 15 years of experience in contract management within the oil &amp; gas, EPCIC, infrastructure, or energy sector.
	Minimum of 5 years in a senior commercial or contract leadership role.
	Demonstrated experience managing high value offshore / onshore EPC projects and complex subcontracting structures.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@oilservltd-ng.com and copy: omjconsult42@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
